clusters.title: IEA agrees to release record volume of emergency oil reserves in effort to calm prices
clusters.dek: The Paris-based organization said it will make 400 million barrels of oil available from its members' emergency reserves.

articles.title: IEA agrees to release record volume of emergency oil reserves in effort to calm prices
articles.summary: The Paris-based organization said it will make 400 million barrels of oil available from its members' emergency reserves.

articles.title: Countries agree to record release of emergency oil reserves as prices surge
articles.summary: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

articles.title: Countries agree to a historic release of stockpiled oil to ease global disruption
articles.summary: Members of the International Energy Agency have announced a coordinated release of 400 million barrels of stockpiled oil in an attempt to counter the disruption in oil trade triggered by the Iran war.

clusters.title: French aid worker among three killed in drone strikes in rebel-held DR Congo city
clusters.dek: Witnesses say drone strikes hit a residential building frequently occupied by expatriates and aid workers in Goma.

articles.title: French aid worker among three killed in drone strikes in rebel-held DR Congo city
articles.summary: Witnesses say drone strikes hit a residential building frequently occupied by expatriates and aid workers in Goma.

articles.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
articles.summary: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

clusters.title: Americans skeptical of the Iran war, poll says. And, DOJ gives guns back to felons
clusters.dek: A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.

articles.title: Americans skeptical of the Iran war, poll says. And, DOJ gives guns back to felons
articles.summary: A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.

articles.title: The Trump DOJ is giving guns back to felons, including one alleged fake elector
articles.summary: The Department of Justice is quietly restarting a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One of them was an alleged fake elector in 2020.

clusters.title: Americans are split on wanting the National Guard to monitor voting, a new poll finds
clusters.dek: Nearly half of Americans support the National Guard monitoring November's elections, potentially signaling an openness to the sort of nationalizing of elections that President Trump says he wants.

articles.title: Americans are split on wanting the National Guard to monitor voting, a new poll finds
articles.summary: Nearly half of Americans support the National Guard monitoring November's elections, potentially signaling an openness to the sort of nationalizing of elections that President Trump says he wants.

articles.title: Americans are increasingly worried about voting, new poll shows
articles.summary: Americans' confidence that their elections will be run fairly has dropped to its lowest point in years, according to a new PBS News/NPR/Marist poll.

clusters.title: Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind
clusters.dek: One of the seven women who received visas has reversed her decision after speaking to teammates, minister says

articles.title: Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind
articles.summary: One of the seven women who received visas has reversed her decision after speaking to teammates, minister says

articles.title: Australia grants asylum to 2 more members of Iranian women's soccer team
articles.summary: Two more members of the Iranian women's soccer team were granted asylum in Australia before their teammates departed, the country's Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said Wednesday.

clusters.title: Man taken into custody after driving van into White House security gate
clusters.dek: The Secret Service said the man crashed into the temporary security barrier just before 6:30 a.m. He was immediately arrested by officers from the Secret Service's uniformed division, the agency said.

articles.title: Man taken into custody after driving van into White House security gate
articles.summary: The Secret Service said the man crashed into the temporary security barrier just before 6:30 a.m. He was immediately arrested by officers from the Secret Service's uniformed division, the agency said.

clusters.title: No Nobles Day: Britain's Parliament boots its last hereditary Lords after 700 years
clusters.dek: Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.

articles.title: No Nobles Day: Britain's Parliament boots its last hereditary Lords after 700 years
articles.summary: Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.

clusters.title: The Iran war and surging oil prices are affecting consumers. Here's how
clusters.dek: You don't have to drive a car to be affected.

clusters.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
clusters.dek: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

clusters.title: British prime minister was warned of 'reputational risk' in appointing Mandelson, files show
clusters.dek: Concerns were raised in a document sent to Prime Minister Keir Starmer in December 2024 when he was considering appointing Peter Mandelson as U.S. ambassador because of his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
